006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with

008 *

010 *

016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.commons.inject;
018
019import java.util.function.*;
020
021/**
022 * Extends {@link BeanStore} with methods for adding beans.
023 *
024 * <p>
025 * This interface provides write operations for bean stores, allowing beans to be added
026 * either as direct instances or as suppliers for lazy initialization.
027 *
028 * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
029 * <p class='bjava'>
030 *    WritableBeanStore <jv>beanStore</jv> = ...;
031 *
032 *    <jc>// Add unnamed bean</jc>
033 *    <jv>beanStore</jv>.addBean(MyService.<jk>class</jk>, <jk>new</jk> MyService());
034 *
035 *    <jc>// Add named bean</jc>
036 *    <jv>beanStore</jv>.addBean(MyService.<jk>class</jk>, <jk>new</jk> MyService(), <js>"primary"</js>);
037 *
038 *    <jc>// Add bean supplier for lazy initialization</jc>
039 *    <jv>beanStore</jv>.addSupplier(MyService.<jk>class</jk>, () -&gt; <jk>new</jk> MyService());
040 *
041 *    <jc>// Add named bean supplier</jc>
042 *    <jv>beanStore</jv>.addSupplier(MyService.<jk>class</jk>, () -&gt; <jk>new</jk> MyService(), <js>"secondary"</js>);
043 *
044 *    <jc>// Clear all beans</jc>
045 *    <jv>beanStore</jv>.clear();
046 * </p>
047 *
048 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
049 *    <li class='jc'>{@link BeanStore} - Read-only bean lookup interface
050 * </ul>
051 */
052public interface WritableBeanStore extends BeanStore, AutoCloseable {
053
054   /**
055    * Same as {@link #addBean(Class,Object)} but returns the bean instead of this object for fluent calls.
056    *
057    * @param <T> The bean type.
058    * @param beanType The bean type.
059    * @param bean The bean instance.  Can be <jk>null</jk>.
060    * @return The bean.
061    */
062   @SuppressWarnings({
063      "resource" // addBean returns this; the discarded return is the store the caller already holds
064   })
065   default <T> T add(Class<T> beanType, T bean) {
066      addBean(beanType, bean);
067      return bean;
068   }
069
070   /**
071    * Same as {@link #addBean(Class,Object,String)} but returns the bean instead of this object for fluent calls.
072    *
073    * @param <T> The bean type.
074    * @param beanType The bean type.
075    * @param bean The bean instance.  Can be <jk>null</jk>.
076    * @param name The bean name.  Can be <jk>null</jk> for unnamed beans.
077    * @return The bean.
078    */
079   @SuppressWarnings({
080      "resource" // addBean returns this; the discarded return is the store the caller already holds
081   })
082   default <T> T add(Class<T> beanType, T bean, String name) {
083      addBean(beanType, bean, name);
084      return bean;
085   }
086
087   /**
088    * Adds an unnamed bean of the specified type to this store.
089    *
090    * @param <T> The bean type.
091    * @param beanType The bean type.
092    * @param bean The bean instance.  Can be <jk>null</jk>.
093    * @return This object for method chaining.
094    */
095   <T> WritableBeanStore addBean(Class<T> beanType, T bean);
096
097   /**
098    * Adds a named bean of the specified type to this store.
099    *
100    * @param <T> The bean type.
101    * @param beanType The bean type.
102    * @param bean The bean instance.  Can be <jk>null</jk>.
103    * @param name The bean name.  Can be <jk>null</jk> for unnamed beans.
104    * @return This object for method chaining.
105    */
106   <T> WritableBeanStore addBean(Class<T> beanType, T bean, String name);
107
108   /**
109    * Adds a supplier for an unnamed bean of the specified type to this store.
110    *
111    * <p>
112    * The supplier will be invoked lazily when the bean is first requested.
113    *
114    * @param <T> The bean type.
115    * @param beanType The bean type.
116    * @param supplier The bean supplier.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
117    * @return This object for method chaining.
118    */
119   <T> WritableBeanStore addSupplier(Class<T> beanType, Supplier<T> supplier);
120
121   /**
122    * Adds a supplier for a named bean of the specified type to this store.
123    *
124    * <p>
125    * The supplier will be invoked lazily when the bean is first requested.
126    *
127    * @param <T> The bean type.
128    * @param beanType The bean type.
129    * @param supplier The bean supplier.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
130    * @param name The bean name.  Can be <jk>null</jk> for unnamed beans.
131    * @return This object for method chaining.
132    */
133   <T> WritableBeanStore addSupplier(Class<T> beanType, Supplier<T> supplier, String name);
134
135   /**
136    * Adds a fallback supplier for an unnamed bean of the specified type to this store.
137    *
138    * <p>
139    * Default suppliers are consulted only after regular {@linkplain #addBean(Class,Object) entries} and the regular {@linkplain BasicBeanStore#BasicBeanStore(BeanStore) parent}
140    * chain have been searched.  They serve as a "use this if nothing else has provided one" hook,
141    * useful for memoizer-backed framework defaults that should not shadow explicit user
142    * registrations or beans inherited from an overriding parent (e.g. a Spring application context).
143    *
144    * @param <T> The bean type.
145    * @param beanType The bean type.
146    * @param supplier The bean supplier.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
147    * @return This object for method chaining.
148    */
149   <T> WritableBeanStore addDefaultSupplier(Class<T> beanType, Supplier<T> supplier);
150
151   /**
152    * Adds a fallback supplier for a named bean of the specified type to this store.
153    *
154    * <p>
155    * Default suppliers are consulted only after regular {@linkplain #addBean(Class,Object,String) entries} and the regular
156    * parent chain have been searched.  See {@link #addDefaultSupplier(Class,Supplier)}.
157    *
158    * @param <T> The bean type.
159    * @param beanType The bean type.
160    * @param supplier The bean supplier.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
161    * @param name The bean name.  Can be <jk>null</jk> for unnamed beans.
162    * @return This object for method chaining.
163    */
164   <T> WritableBeanStore addDefaultSupplier(Class<T> beanType, Supplier<T> supplier, String name);
165
166   /**
167    * Removes all beans from this store.
168    *
169    * <p>
170    * This operation only affects this store and does not affect any parent stores
171    * that may be associated with this store.
172    *
173    * @return This object for method chaining.
174    */
175   WritableBeanStore clear();
176
177   /**
178    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this store has a {@linkplain #addDefaultSupplier(Class,Supplier) default supplier}
179    * registered locally for the specified unnamed bean type.
180    *
181    * <p>
182    * This is intended for callers that need to distinguish "framework default present" from "any binding
183    * exists" — for example, REST initialization uses this signal to skip types that are managed by an
184    * internal memoizer.  Parent and overriding-parent stores are <i>not</i> consulted.
185    *
186    * @param beanType The bean type to check.
187    * @return <jk>true</jk> if a default supplier for the unnamed bean type is registered on this store.
188    */
189   boolean hasDefaultSupplier(Class<?> beanType);
190
191   /**
192    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this store has a {@linkplain #addDefaultSupplier(Class,Supplier,String) default supplier}
193    * registered locally for the specified bean type and name.
194    *
195    * <p>
196    * See {@link #hasDefaultSupplier(Class)} for the rationale.  Parent and overriding-parent stores are
197    * <i>not</i> consulted.
198    *
199    * @param beanType The bean type to check.
200    * @param name The bean name.  Can be <jk>null</jk> for unnamed beans.
201    * @return <jk>true</jk> if a default supplier for the bean type and name is registered on this store.
202    */
203   boolean hasDefaultSupplier(Class<?> beanType, String name);
204
205   /**
206    * Registers a default implementation class for the specified bean type.
207    *
208    * <p>
209    * Type bindings are consulted by callers (notably {@link BeanInstantiator} and the various REST
210    * memoizers) when resolving the impl class to instantiate for <c>beanType</c>.  This is the
211    * type-binding analogue of {@link #addDefaultSupplier(Class,Supplier) addDefaultSupplier}: it
212    * provides a deferred-construction default that can be overridden by later resolution steps
213    * (e.g. an annotation override).
214    *
215    * @param <T> The bean type.
216    * @param beanType The bean type to bind.
217    * @param implType The implementation class to instantiate when this type is resolved.
218    * @return This object for method chaining.
219    */
220   <T> WritableBeanStore addBeanType(Class<T> beanType, Class<? extends T> implType);
221
222   /**
223    * Registers a configuration class and its {@link Bean}-annotated members.
224    *
225    * @param configType The configuration type.
226    * @return This object.
227    */
228   WritableBeanStore registerConfiguration(Class<?> configType);
229
230   /**
231    * Registers multiple configuration classes.
232    *
233    * @param configTypes The configuration types.
234    * @return This object.
235    */
236   @SuppressWarnings({
237      "resource" // registerConfiguration returns this; the discarded return is the store the caller already holds
238   })
239   default WritableBeanStore registerConfigurations(Class<?>... configTypes) {
240      if (configTypes != null)
241         for (var c : configTypes)
242            registerConfiguration(c);
243      return this;
244   }
245
246   /**
247    * Pushes the supplied {@link BeanStore} onto this store's overlay stack.
248    *
249    * <p>
250    * The pushed frame becomes the top-priority lookup target for subsequent bean resolution &mdash; it sits above
251    * any pre-existing {@code overridingParent} setting, this store's local entries, the regular parent chain, and
252    * the default-supplier tier.  Frames are popped in LIFO order via {@link #popOverlay(Snapshot)}.
253    *
254    * <p>
255    * This is the framework primitive behind <b>Mode OVERLAY</b> of the test-injection model: a long-lived SUT's
256    * bean store has a per-test overlay pushed at {@code @BeforeEach}, then popped at {@code @AfterEach}, so
257    * subsequent tests see the original beans again with no rebuild cost.
258    *
259    * <h5 class='section'>Composition with {@code overridingParent}:</h5>
260    * <p>
261    * When this store was constructed with an
262    * {@linkplain BasicBeanStore#BasicBeanStore(BeanStore, BeanStore) overriding parent} <i>and</i> has overlays
263    * pushed on top, the overlay stack wins (it is "more recent").  Both are still consulted before local entries
264    * and the regular parent.
265    *
266    * @param overlay The bean store to layer on top.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
267    * @return A {@link Snapshot} that must be passed back to {@link #popOverlay(Snapshot)} to remove the frame.
268    * @throws NullPointerException If {@code overlay} is <jk>null</jk>.
269    * @since 10.0.0
270    */
271   Snapshot pushOverlay(BeanStore overlay);
272
273   /**
274    * Removes the overlay frame identified by the supplied {@link Snapshot}.
275    *
276    * <p>
277    * Pops must be issued in <b>LIFO</b> order &mdash; the supplied snapshot must identify the current top-of-stack
278    * frame.  Out-of-order pops, pops on an empty stack, and pops of a snapshot produced by a different store all
279    * throw {@link IllegalStateException} with a descriptive message.  These are programming errors, surfaced loudly
280    * rather than silently degraded.
281    *
282    * @param snapshot The snapshot returned by a prior {@link #pushOverlay(BeanStore)} call.  Must not be
283    *    <jk>null</jk>.
284    * @throws NullPointerException If {@code snapshot} is <jk>null</jk>.
285    * @throws IllegalStateException If the snapshot was produced by a different store, the overlay stack is empty,
286    *    or the snapshot does not identify the top-of-stack frame (LIFO violation).
287    * @since 10.0.0
288    */
289   void popOverlay(Snapshot snapshot);
290
291   /**
292    * Closes this bean store, invoking pre-destroy callbacks.
293    *
294    * @throws BeanCreationException on shutdown failures.
295    */
296   @Override
297   void close() throws BeanCreationException;
298}
299
